introduction.p: Simplify these whole number and fraction products.

solution.p: Multiplying the 5 with the numerator 2 gives a new numerator 10\text{.} We keep the denominator 9 and the answer is \frac{10}{9}\text{.}

Before we multiply, we can do some simplfication first. We want to make \frac{12\cdot7}{3}\text{,} but now we can cancel a factor of 3\text{.} We have, \frac{\cancelto{12}{4}\cdot7}{\cancelto{3}{1}}\text{.} So we have \frac{28}{1}\text{,} or just 28\text{.}

Before we multiply, we can do some simplfication first. We want to make \frac{14\cdot5}{6}\text{,} but now we can cancel a factor of 2\text{.} We have, \frac{\cancelto{14}{7}\cdot5}{\cancelto{6}{3}}\text{.} So we have \frac{35}{3}\text{.}
